resources.string: Late Heart Rate Nadir
Your heart rate nadir occurred later than usual last night, suggesting delayed recovery.
Potential Illness Detected
Elevated resting heart rate and suppressed HRV indicate your body may be fighting something off.
Strong Recovery Signal
Your HRV and resting heart rate were unusually favorable compared with your baseline.
Training Load May Be Affecting Recovery
Your recent training load is high, and your recovery markers show signs of strain.
Your recent daily physiological load is high, and your recovery markers show signs of strain.
Restore dismissed insights (%1$d)
Dismiss health insight
Restore dismissed health insights
Workout Impact
Yesterday\'s high strain workout appears to be carrying into today because both HRV and RHR are outside their optimal ranges.
Rest Day Success
Your dedicated rest day resulted in an optimal HRV rebound.
Your night\'s rest was perfect!
Rest Day
Your rest day did not lead to a significant HRV rebound.
Circadian Shift Delayed Recovery

plurals.name: insight_circadian_shift_body
plurals.item: No recovery signal triggered despite a rest day. This correlates with a bedtime %1$d min later than your average, which shifted your circadian rhythm and impacted overnight recovery.
No recovery signal triggered despite a rest day. This correlates with a bedtime %1$d min later than your average, which shifted your circadian rhythm and impacted overnight recovery.

resources.string: High Strain and Sleep Deficit

plurals.name: insight_high_strain_sleep_deficit_body
plurals.item: Your acute strain ratio of %1$.1f combined with %2$d minute less sleep than your goal is suppressing your recovery.
Your acute strain ratio of %1$.1f combined with %2$d minutes less sleep than your goal is suppressing your recovery.

resources.string: Late Recovery and Short Sleep

plurals.name: insight_late_nadir_short_sleep_body
plurals.item: Your heart rate nadir occurred late in the night, and you slept %1$d minute less than your %2$d minute goal, limiting overnight restoration.
Your heart rate nadir occurred late in the night, and you slept %1$d minutes less than your %2$d minute goal, limiting overnight restoration.

resources.string: HRV Data Missing
Today\'s readiness score is based on resting heart rate and sleep only because no HRV reading was recorded last night.
Sleep Stage Data Missing
Sleep stage data wasn\'t recorded last night, so your sleep score reflects duration and restoration only, not architecture.
Possible Illness or Breathing Disruption
Your HRV dropped sharply to a z-score of %1$.1f and your average sleeping blood oxygen fell to %2$.0f%%, which can indicate the onset of illness or a breathing disruption such as sleep apnea.
Delayed Recovery with Elevated Resting Heart Rate
Your heart rate nadir was delayed and your resting heart rate was %1$.1f bpm above baseline, suggesting your body is still working to recover.
Elevated Blood Pressure Under High Strain
Your systolic blood pressure is %1$d mmHg above your recent baseline while your strain ratio of %2$.1f indicates high training load.
RAS Depleted Despite Training Load
Your RAS score of %1$.0f is low even though your strain ratio of %2$.1f shows a high training load, suggesting your effort isn\'t translating into RAS gains.
HRV Below Baseline Multiple Nights

plurals.name: insight_hrv_decline_streak_body
plurals.item: Your HRV has been below your baseline for %1$d consecutive night, which can signal accumulating fatigue.
Your HRV has been below your baseline for %1$d consecutive nights, which can signal accumulating fatigue.

resources.string: Low Daily Activity

plurals.name: insight_step_shortfall_body
plurals.item: You took %1$d step today, well short of your %2$d step goal.
You took %1$d steps today, well short of your %2$d step goal.

resources.string: Weekly RAS Below Target
Your rolling weekly RAS total of %1$.0f is below the recommended target of %2$.0f.
Weight Change Under High Training Load
Your weight has changed by %1$.1f kg (%2$.1f%%) over the past week while your training load is high, which can indicate fluid shifts or under-fueling.
Observed Signal
What This Might Mean
Confidence
Low
Low-Medium
Medium
Medium-High
High
Why This Might Have Happened
What You Can Do Today
What Not To Infer
Safety Note
Why This Matters
What Might Explain It
What You Can Do
What Data Is Missing
How This Affects Your Score
What You Can Check
Show explanation for %1$s
Your systolic blood pressure is more than 10 mmHg above your recent baseline, and your strain ratio is high.
Your cardiovascular system may be under additional stress today.
If blood pressure is repeatedly high, very high, or occurs with chest pain, shortness of breath, severe headache, dizziness, or neurological symptoms, seek medical advice.

string-array.name: insight_detail_bp_elevated_high_strain_causes
string-array.item: High recent training load|Recent training may be adding cardiovascular demand.|HIGH_STRAIN_RATIO
Poor sleep|Short or disrupted sleep can make recovery signals less favorable.|POOR_SLEEP
Stress|Mental or physical stress can affect blood pressure and recovery markers.|GENERIC
Caffeine or stimulants|Recent stimulant intake can raise blood pressure in some people.|GENERIC
Dehydration|Fluid balance can influence heart rate and blood pressure.|GENERIC
Alcohol|Alcohol can disrupt sleep and overnight recovery.|GENERIC
Measurement timing or device variation|Blood pressure readings vary with posture, timing, cuff fit, and device noise.|GENERIC

string-array.name: insight_detail_bp_elevated_high_strain_recommendations
string-array.item: Avoid very intense training if blood pressure remains elevated or you feel unwell.
Retake the measurement under consistent resting conditions.
Prioritize hydration, sleep, and recovery.

string-array.name: insight_detail_bp_elevated_high_strain_caveats
string-array.item: One elevated reading does not diagnose hypertension.

resources.string: You went to bed more than 90 minutes later than your usual bedtime, and no clear recovery rebound was detected after a rest day.
A large bedtime shift can make sleep and recovery signals less consistent.

string-array.name: insight_detail_circadian_shift_recovery_miss_causes
string-array.item: Later bedtime|Your sleep timing moved well outside your usual pattern.|LARGE_BEDTIME_SHIFT
Shorter sleep opportunity|Less time in bed can reduce recovery opportunity.|POOR_SLEEP
Social schedule or travel|Schedule changes can shift sleep timing.|GENERIC
Stress|Stress can delay sleep onset and affect overnight physiology.|GENERIC
Late meal or screen exposure|Evening routines can delay sleep and recovery signals.|GENERIC
Normal HRV variability|One night can move around because of normal measurement variation.|GENERIC

string-array.name: insight_detail_circadian_shift_recovery_miss_recommendations
string-array.item: Try to return to your usual sleep schedule tonight.
Keep training flexible if you also feel tired.

string-array.name: insight_detail_circadian_shift_recovery_miss_caveats
string-array.item: This does not prove your circadian rhythm shifted.

resources.string: Your strain ratio is high and you slept less than 85% of your goal.
Your body may have had less overnight recovery time after a high-load period.

string-array.name: insight_detail_high_strain_sleep_deficit_causes
string-array.item: High training load|Recent training load is above your usual range.|HIGH_STRAIN_RATIO
Short sleep duration|You slept well below your configured sleep goal.|POOR_SLEEP
Late workout|Training close to bedtime can delay overnight settling.|LATE_WORKOUT
Stress|Stress can reduce sleep quality and recovery consistency.|GENERIC
Poor sleep timing|A shifted sleep window can make recovery less predictable.|POOR_SLEEP
Under-fueling|Not eating enough can make high-load periods harder to recover from.|GENERIC

string-array.name: insight_detail_high_strain_sleep_deficit_recommendations
string-array.item: Avoid adding another hard session if you feel tired.
Prioritize an earlier bedtime tonight.
Consider easy training or recovery work.

string-array.name: insight_detail_high_strain_sleep_deficit_caveats
string-array.item: This does not prove your recovery is impaired.

resources.string: Your HRV has been below your baseline for three consecutive nights.
A multi-night HRV decline may reflect increased recovery demand, stress, illness, or poor sleep.

string-array.name: insight_detail_hrv_decline_streak_causes
string-array.item: Accumulated training load|Repeated training stress can increase recovery demand.|HIGH_STRAIN_RATIO
Poor sleep|Sleep loss can lower HRV relative to baseline.|POOR_SLEEP
Stress|Mental or physical stress can lower HRV.|GENERIC
Illness onset|Early illness can appear as lower HRV and higher resting heart rate.|GENERIC
Alcohol|Alcohol often disrupts sleep and autonomic recovery.|GENERIC
Dehydration|Fluid loss can affect HRV and heart rate.|GENERIC
Travel|Travel can disrupt timing, sleep, hydration, and recovery.|GENERIC

string-array.name: insight_detail_hrv_decline_streak_recommendations
string-array.item: Look at how you feel and whether resting heart rate, sleep, or readiness also look worse.
If several markers are poor, consider reducing intensity.

string-array.name: insight_detail_hrv_decline_streak_caveats
string-array.item: HRV below baseline does not identify the exact cause.

resources.string: Your HRV was much lower than usual and your average sleeping oxygen was below 94%.
This combination can occur with illness, poor recovery, altitude, congestion, device fit issues, or breathing-related sleep disruption.
Repeated low oxygen readings, loud snoring, pauses in breathing, or strong daytime sleepiness are worth discussing with a clinician.

string-array.name: insight_detail_hrv_drop_low_spo2_causes
string-array.item: Early illness or congestion|Congestion or early illness can affect breathing and HRV.|GENERIC
Poor sleep|Disrupted sleep can lower HRV and affect oxygen readings.|POOR_SLEEP
Breathing disruption|Overnight breathing disruption can appear as lower oxygen.|GENERIC
Sleeping position|Position can affect breathing and sensor contact.|GENERIC
Altitude|Higher altitude can lower overnight oxygen saturation.|GENERIC
Device fit or sensor noise|Wearable oxygen readings can be sensitive to fit and motion.|GENERIC

string-array.name: insight_detail_hrv_drop_low_spo2_recommendations
string-array.item: Check how you feel and whether low oxygen repeats on other nights.
Prioritize rest if you also feel sick or unusually fatigued.

string-array.name: insight_detail_hrv_drop_low_spo2_caveats
string-array.item: This does not diagnose sleep apnea or any medical condition.

resources.string: Your lowest heart rate happened in the final third of your sleep period.
Your body may have taken longer than usual to settle into a more recovered overnight state.

string-array.name: insight_detail_late_nadir_causes
string-array.item: Late workout|Training close to bedtime can delay overnight settling.|LATE_WORKOUT
High training load|High load can extend overnight recovery demand.|HIGH_STRAIN_RATIO
Late meal|Eating late can keep heart rate elevated.|GENERIC
Alcohol|Alcohol can delay overnight heart-rate recovery.|GENERIC
Stress|Stress can keep heart rate elevated during sleep.|GENERIC
Travel|Travel can disrupt timing and recovery.|GENERIC
Poor sleep|Fragmented sleep can alter heart-rate patterns.|POOR_SLEEP
Early illness|Early illness can raise overnight heart rate.|GENERIC

string-array.name: insight_detail_late_nadir_recommendations
string-array.item: Keep today flexible. If you feel tired, prefer easy training, mobility, or recovery work.

string-array.name: insight_detail_late_nadir_caveats
string-array.item: A late heart rate nadir does not prove poor recovery or identify a single cause.

resources.string: Your lowest heart rate occurred late, and your resting heart rate was more than 5 bpm above baseline.
Your body may have remained under physiological strain during the night.

string-array.name: insight_detail_late_nadir_elevated_rhr_causes
string-array.item: Late or intense workout|Hard training or late timing can keep heart rate elevated overnight.|GENERIC
Illness onset|Early illness can raise resting heart rate.|GENERIC
Poor sleep|Short or disrupted sleep can reduce overnight recovery.|POOR_SLEEP
Alcohol|Alcohol can raise overnight heart rate.|GENERIC
Dehydration|Fluid loss can raise heart rate.|GENERIC
Stress|Stress can increase overnight physiological strain.|GENERIC
Heat|Heat exposure can raise heart rate and recovery demand.|GENERIC

string-array.name: insight_detail_late_nadir_elevated_rhr_recommendations
string-array.item: Choose an easier training option if you also feel tired or unwell.
Prioritize hydration and sleep tonight.

string-array.name: insight_detail_late_nadir_elevated_rhr_caveats
string-array.item: This does not diagnose illness or prove overtraining.

resources.string: Your heart rate nadir occurred late and you slept less than 85% of your sleep goal.
Your recovery window may have been shortened before your body fully settled overnight.

string-array.name: insight_detail_late_nadir_short_sleep_causes
string-array.item: Short sleep duration|You slept well below your configured sleep goal.|POOR_SLEEP
Late bedtime|A late bedtime can reduce sleep opportunity.|GENERIC
Early wake-up|An early wake-up can end sleep before full recovery opportunity.|GENERIC
Late workout|Training close to bedtime can delay overnight settling.|LATE_WORKOUT
Stress|Stress can fragment sleep and keep heart rate higher.|GENERIC
Poor sleep continuity|Interrupted sleep can reduce recovery opportunity.|POOR_SLEEP

string-array.name: insight_detail_late_nadir_short_sleep_recommendations
string-array.item: Keep intensity flexible today and aim for more sleep opportunity tonight.

string-array.name: insight_detail_late_nadir_short_sleep_caveats
string-array.item: This does not prove your recovery was poor, but it suggests less recovery opportunity.

resources.string: Your recent training load is above your usual range, and at least one recovery marker is less favorable than normal.
Your body may need more recovery before another hard session.
If fatigue persists despite several easy days, or if performance drops for a longer period, consider adjusting your training plan or seeking professional guidance.

string-array.name: insight_detail_load_spike_recovery_strain_causes
string-array.item: High strain ratio|Your acute training strain is high compared with your longer-term baseline.|HIGH_STRAIN_RATIO
High TRIMP yesterday|Yesterday\'s training load was high.|HIGH_TRIMP_YESTERDAY
Short sleep|You slept well below your configured sleep goal.|POOR_SLEEP
Low HRV|Your HRV is below your recent baseline.|LOW_HRV
Elevated resting heart rate|Your resting heart rate is above your recent baseline.|ELEVATED_RHR
Dehydration|Fluid loss can make recovery markers less favorable.|GENERIC
Under-fueling|Not eating enough can make high-load periods harder to recover from.|GENERIC
Heat stress|Heat can raise cardiovascular strain and recovery demand.|GENERIC
General life stress|Life stress can affect HRV, resting heart rate, and sleep.|GENERIC

string-array.name: insight_detail_load_spike_recovery_strain_recommendations
string-array.item: Avoid stacking another hard session on top of this signal.
Prefer easy aerobic work, mobility, or rest if you also feel tired.
Prioritize sleep, hydration, and enough food before your next hard session.

string-array.name: insight_detail_load_spike_recovery_strain_caveats
string-array.item: This does not indicate overtraining.
This does not predict injury by itself.

resources.string: Your RAS score is below target while your strain ratio indicates recent training load.
Some workouts may add training strain without raising RAS much, depending on heart-rate intensity and duration.

string-array.name: insight_detail_ras_depletion_high_strain_causes
string-array.item: Strength training with lower sustained heart rate|RAS rewards sustained heart-rate activity more than brief spikes.|GENERIC
Short workouts|Short sessions may not add much RAS even when they feel hard.|GENERIC
Incomplete heart-rate data|Missing heart-rate samples can reduce RAS credit.|GENERIC
Training intensity below RAS zones|Some work may be below zones that contribute strongly to RAS.|GENERIC
Device measurement gaps|Wearable gaps can affect RAS calculation.|GENERIC

string-array.name: insight_detail_ras_depletion_high_strain_recommendations
string-array.item: If RAS is a goal, include some sustained aerobic work in appropriate heart-rate zones.

string-array.name: insight_detail_ras_depletion_high_strain_caveats
string-array.item: Low RAS does not mean your training was useless.

resources.string: Your 7-day RAS total is below the configured weekly target.
RAS is designed to reward sustained heart-rate activity over the week.

string-array.name: insight_detail_ras_weekly_underperformance_causes
string-array.item: Too few aerobic sessions|Fewer sustained sessions can lower weekly RAS.|GENERIC
Low heart-rate intensity|Lower-intensity sessions may add less RAS.|GENERIC
Missing heart-rate data|Missing heart-rate samples can reduce RAS credit.|GENERIC
Recovery week|A planned easier week can reduce weekly RAS.|GENERIC
Strength-focused training|Strength work may add less RAS than sustained aerobic work.|GENERIC

string-array.name: insight_detail_ras_weekly_underperformance_recommendations
string-array.item: Add easy or moderate aerobic sessions if this fits your training plan.

string-array.name: insight_detail_ras_weekly_underperformance_caveats
string-array.item: A low RAS week is not automatically bad if you are intentionally recovering or focusing on strength.

resources.string: No sleep HRV reading was recorded last night.
Readiness may be less personalized today because HRV could not be included.

string-array.name: insight_detail_recovery_hrv_missing_causes
string-array.item: Watch not worn overnight|No overnight wear can prevent HRV capture.|GENERIC
Loose fit|Poor sensor contact can prevent HRV capture.|GENERIC
Low battery|Low battery can stop overnight recording.|GENERIC
Sync delay|Health Connect data may not have arrived yet.|GENERIC
Device did not record HRV|Some devices or nights may not include HRV.|GENERIC
Health Connect did not provide HRV data|The source app may not have shared HRV data.|GENERIC

string-array.name: insight_detail_recovery_hrv_missing_recommendations
string-array.item: Check whether your wearable recorded HRV and whether Health Connect synced overnight data.

resources.string: Sleep stage coverage was missing or suspicious last night.
Sleep score may be less complete because sleep architecture could not be evaluated.

string-array.name: insight_detail_recovery_stages_missing_causes
string-array.item: Watch not worn consistently|Inconsistent wear can reduce stage coverage.|GENERIC
Loose fit|Poor sensor contact can prevent sleep staging.|GENERIC
Device did not classify sleep stages|Some nights may not receive stage labels.|GENERIC
Short or interrupted sleep|Short or fragmented sleep can reduce stage coverage.|GENERIC
Sync delay|Health Connect data may not have arrived yet.|GENERIC
Health Connect data gap|The source app may not have shared stage data.|GENERIC

string-array.name: insight_detail_recovery_stages_missing_recommendations
string-array.item: Check your wearable sleep report and Health Connect sync status.

resources.string: Yesterday was a low-load day, but today\'s HRV did not clearly improve.
One light day may not have been enough to shift your recovery markers, or this may simply reflect normal HRV variability.

string-array.name: insight_detail_rest_day_no_impact_causes
string-array.item: Accumulated fatigue|Recovery markers can take more than one day to rebound.|GENERIC
Poor sleep|Poor sleep can blunt a recovery rebound.|POOR_SLEEP
Stress|Stress can keep HRV lower despite low training load.|GENERIC
Dehydration|Fluid loss can affect HRV.|GENERIC
Early illness|Early illness can lower HRV.|GENERIC
Normal HRV variation|One morning can vary because of normal measurement noise.|GENERIC

string-array.name: insight_detail_rest_day_no_impact_recommendations
string-array.item: Watch the next 2–3 days rather than reacting to one morning.

string-array.name: insight_detail_rest_day_no_impact_caveats
string-array.item: This does not mean the rest day failed.

resources.string: Yesterday was a low-load day and today\'s HRV improved.
Your body may have responded positively to lower training stress.

string-array.name: insight_detail_rest_day_success_causes
string-array.item: Reduced training load|Lower load can support recovery.|HIGH_STRAIN_RATIO
Good sleep|Good sleep can improve recovery markers.|GENERIC
Lower stress|Lower stress can support HRV.|GENERIC
Hydration|Better fluid balance can support recovery signals.|GENERIC
Normal HRV variability|One strong morning can reflect normal variation.|GENERIC

string-array.name: insight_detail_rest_day_success_recommendations
string-array.item: Treat this as encouraging, not a guarantee.

string-array.name: insight_detail_rest_day_success_caveats
string-array.item: This does not prove you are fully recovered or guaranteed to perform well.

resources.string: Your resting heart rate is higher than usual and your HRV is lower than usual for more than one day.
This pattern can occur with illness, inflammation, poor sleep, high stress, or heavy recovery demand.
If you have fever, chest pain, shortness of breath, fainting, severe symptoms, or persistent abnormal readings, seek medical advice.

string-array.name: insight_detail_sick_indicator_causes
string-array.item: Early illness|Early illness can lower HRV and raise resting heart rate.|GENERIC
Poor sleep|Short or disrupted sleep can make recovery markers less favorable.|POOR_SLEEP
High stress|Stress can lower HRV and raise resting heart rate.|GENERIC
Heavy training|Heavy training can increase recovery demand.|GENERIC
Dehydration|Fluid loss can raise heart rate.|GENERIC
Alcohol|Alcohol can disrupt sleep and recovery markers.|GENERIC
Heat|Heat can raise heart rate and strain.|GENERIC
Travel|Travel can disrupt sleep, hydration, and timing.|GENERIC

string-array.name: insight_detail_sick_indicator_recommendations
string-array.item: Reduce intensity today, prioritize sleep and hydration, and check how you feel.

string-array.name: insight_detail_sick_indicator_caveats
string-array.item: This does not diagnose an infection or identify the cause of the signal.

resources.string: Your step count is below 70% of your daily goal.
Daily movement supports general activity balance, but a low-step day may be intentional during recovery.

string-array.name: insight_detail_step_shortfall_causes
string-array.item: Recovery day|Lower movement may be intentional during recovery.|GENERIC
Desk-heavy day|Long seated work can reduce step count.|GENERIC
Travel|Travel can reduce normal movement.|GENERIC
Missed device wear|Steps can be undercounted if the device was not worn.|GENERIC
Low outdoor activity|Less outdoor time often reduces steps.|GENERIC
Intentional taper|A taper can intentionally reduce daily movement.|GENERIC

string-array.name: insight_detail_step_shortfall_recommendations
string-array.item: If it fits your day, a short walk can help close the gap.

string-array.name: insight_detail_step_shortfall_caveats
string-array.item: A low step count is not automatically bad, especially on rest or recovery days.

resources.string: Your HRV was much higher than usual and your resting heart rate was much lower than usual.
This pattern is consistent with a favorable overnight recovery signal.

string-array.name: insight_detail_strong_recovery_signal_causes
string-array.item: Good sleep|Good sleep can support favorable recovery markers.|GENERIC
Lower stress|Lower stress can support HRV and resting heart rate.|GENERIC
Low recent training load|Lower load can improve recovery markers.|HIGH_STRAIN_RATIO
Good hydration|Hydration can support cardiovascular recovery.|GENERIC
Normal HRV variability|A strong single signal can reflect normal HRV variation.|GENERIC

string-array.name: insight_detail_strong_recovery_signal_recommendations
string-array.item: This is an encouraging signal. If you also feel good, normal training is reasonable.
Avoid using one strong recovery signal as a reason to suddenly increase training load.

string-array.name: insight_detail_strong_recovery_signal_caveats
string-array.item: This does not guarantee peak performance today.
It does not mean you should automatically train harder than planned.

resources.string: Your weight changed by more than 2% over the past week while training strain is high.
During high training load, short-term weight changes often reflect fluid balance, glycogen, food volume, or recovery stress rather than true tissue change.

string-array.name: insight_detail_weight_drift_training_load_causes
string-array.item: Fluid loss|Sweat and hydration changes can move weight quickly.|GENERIC
Glycogen changes|Stored carbohydrate changes also shift water weight.|GENERIC
Under-fueling|Eating too little during high load can affect weight and recovery.|GENERIC
High sweat loss|Heat or long sessions can cause fluid shifts.|GENERIC
Salt intake changes|Sodium changes can affect water retention.|GENERIC
Meal timing|Food volume and timing can move scale weight.|GENERIC
Scale timing variation|Different weigh-in timing can change readings.|GENERIC

string-array.name: insight_detail_weight_drift_training_load_recommendations
string-array.item: Look at the trend, not one weigh-in.
Make sure you are eating and hydrating enough during high-load periods.

string-array.name: insight_detail_weight_drift_training_load_caveats
string-array.item: A one-week weight change does not prove fat gain or fat loss.

resources.string: Yesterday\'s training load was high, HRV is no longer optimal, RHR is no longer optimal, and HRV dropped meaningfully from yesterday.
Your body may still be repairing, refueling, and returning to baseline after a demanding session.

string-array.name: insight_detail_workout_impact_causes
string-array.item: Long workout|Long duration can raise training load.|GENERIC
High-intensity workout|High intensity can raise TRIMP.|GENERIC
Late workout|Late training can affect overnight recovery.|LATE_WORKOUT
Poor sleep after training|Poor sleep can extend recovery demand.|POOR_SLEEP
Dehydration|Fluid loss can make recovery feel harder.|GENERIC
Under-fueling|Not eating enough can slow recovery from hard sessions.|GENERIC
Heat|Heat can increase cardiovascular strain.|GENERIC

string-array.name: insight_detail_workout_impact_recommendations
string-array.item: Use today as a flexible day. If you feel good, normal training may still be fine; if you feel flat, choose easy training.

string-array.name: insight_detail_workout_impact_caveats
string-array.item: A high TRIMP day does not automatically mean your recovery is poor.
